author: @lisakingscott7729
text:
The hand painted coil is rather low resolution, but it gives me an idea...
Years ago you made copper formate in order to make copper nanoparticles.
You could make a paint / ink with the coper formate and coat the whole film with it. Then using a low cost laser plotter, selectively convert the copper formate to copper. Ideally the unconverted copper formate ink would then be washed off. The copper could be burnished to increase conductivity and electroplating could be used to increase the thickness, if necessary.
Silver, nickel or other metal formate inks could be made with heat/laser conversion properties.
Thinking of which, what a brilliant way to make flex PCBs at home!!! 😁
In fact, this technique could be used to produce multilayer PCBs. After each copper layer is produced and unconverted copper formate removed, it could be sprayed with a suitable insulating lacquer. A mask could be used where layer interconnections would be required, then another layer of formate ink laid down and the process repeated. Polyimide substrate and coatings would be ideal due to their heat resistance. Polyimide would also give the capability of selective laser sintering to produce resistors directly from the polyimide.

text:
would the ink work to paint into grooves of a hand carved drum such that you could run the sound of the drum to a pickup to plug into an amplifier? 

Or even to hookup to a phone or computer to receive the live sound from the drum? i suppose I am thinking of oort of just reversing what you've done here so that the speaker acts as a microphone instead of a speaker & how that can be applied to instruments. Thanks for sharing this & all the rest, Robert, wishing you and yours nothing but the best

author: @hanslepoeter5167
text:
It's a low inductance you've got for that speaker but it doesn't take much power for your ears to hear that. It's more like the sensitivity of your hearing is the amazing part.
I made this crystal radio ( no power source ) when I was young with the piezo headpiece. I modified the headpiece transfering the vibrations to a piece of paper. Out came BBC news  which could be heared on the other side of the room. Remember I live in holland. I remember like yesterday. First this typical tune and then a voice "This is BBC news". Loud and clear. I guess they had a powerfull transmitter too :-).

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: you are quite right the power is minuscule but the potential is huge

author: @jasonhaymanonthedrawingboard
text: Definitely options, considering acoustic levitation is a thing. Aerospace application for sure. Some even nasa would like. Like electric rocket systems.  If impact density is a thing that could be achieved with the right resonance. The whole spaceframe could be completely cover in graphine and be a light as a balloon. It would just need a detonation pulse followed by atmospheric equalisation. 20000 ft in a second would be possible. 8-9Gs of acceleration easy. Scariest ride and wireless sensation. Don’t need fuel just need a short sharp pulse. For a launch vehicle. I suspect it 0.0000001 nano second pulse delivering the rocket energy equivalent.  If you ever wanted to fly to the stars on your favourite tunes now would be that time.
